name: Kai Nakamura
domain: Graduate student (MS in Computer Science, focus on NLP)
communication_style: Casual and self-aware, heavy use of hedging ('I think maybe', 'not sure but'). Alternates between technical precision when discussing research and loose, emoji-adjacent energy in social contexts. Tends to overthink in writing — long messages with parenthetical asides.
emotional_register: Anxious about career path but genuinely curious. Imposter syndrome surfaces around advisor interactions. Relaxed and funny with friends. Vulnerable about whether academia is the right fit.

spaces.description: Graduate coursework, assignments, exams, reading groups, study sessions
Thesis work, NLP experiments, advisor meetings, lab group, paper reading, conference prep
Friends, roommate dynamics, events, dating, hobbies (climbing, board games), social obligations
Job applications, internship search, resume, LinkedIn, interview prep, industry vs academia decision
Mental health, sleep, exercise, therapy, burnout, coping strategies, screen time

cross_space_threads.name: Thesis Deadline ↔ Job Search
key_facts: Thesis defense scheduled for May 2026
Advisor Dr. Reeves wants Kai to submit to ACL 2026 (deadline January 15)
Kai simultaneously applying to industry jobs (ML engineer roles)
Google interview scheduled for February — conflicts with thesis crunch
ACL paper submitted on deadline but Kai unsatisfied with the results section
Google offer received in March: $165K base in Mountain View
Kai decides to accept Google and not pursue PhD
cross_space_threads.temporal_evolution: Oct: both tracks feel manageable → Dec: ACL deadline pressure + first job apps → Jan: submission crunch, sleep suffers → Feb: Google interviews, thesis paused → Mar: offer received, big decision made

cross_space_threads.name: Advisor Expectations ↔ Career Direction
key_facts: Dr. Reeves assumes Kai will pursue a PhD after MS
Kai hasn't told Dr. Reeves about industry applications
Lab mate Preethi hints that Reeves takes it personally when students leave for industry
Kai tells Dr. Reeves about Google offer in March — Reeves is disappointed but supportive
The conversation is awkward but necessary
Reeves agrees to write recommendation letter anyway
cross_space_threads.temporal_evolution: Nov: unspoken tension → Jan: Kai avoids the topic, guilt builds → Feb: Preethi warns Kai → Mar: honest conversation, relief

cross_space_threads.name: Academic Pressure ↔ Social Withdrawal
key_facts: Kai stops going to weekly climbing sessions in December
Roommate Jess notices Kai is isolating — brings it up in January
Kai skips three game nights in a row (Nov-Dec-Jan)
Starts therapy in January via university counseling center
Therapist suggests structured social time as non-negotiable
Kai returns to climbing in February, feels significantly better
cross_space_threads.temporal_evolution: Nov: starting to cancel plans → Dec: full isolation mode → Jan: Jess intervenes, therapy starts → Feb: gradual re-engagement

datapoints.value: literature review done, experiments not started
8/10 — offer in hand, climbing again, clarity
